title: At the end of the month
quote: To number our days is not morbid. It is how we learn to spend them.
body:
Teach us to number our days, that we may get a heart of wisdom. A month is ending. A small threshold. Easy to miss.

I used to wait for the big markers. A new year, a decade, a funeral. As if wisdom only arrived with weight. The verse says something quieter. Count the days. Just the days.

Thirty of them just went by. Some were ordinary. Some were hard. Some you will not remember by Friday. But they were given. That is reason enough to stop and say so.

Today: close the month honestly. Count what was given. Begin tomorrow clean.

title: Not your strength
quote: The strength that actually holds you is not the one you manufactured.
body:
Not by might, not by power, but by my Spirit, says the Lord. The verse is a relief if you let it be one.

I spent a long season trying to rebuild by will. More discipline. More grit. More hours before anyone else was up. Some of that was good. Most of it was me trying to prove to God that I could earn the rebuilding He was already doing for free.

The rebuild that holds is not the one I powered. It is the one I received. Every time I have tried to carry it alone, it has gotten heavier. Every time I have let it be His, it has gotten lighter without being lighter.

Today: notice where you are still trying to lift what was never yours to lift.

title: The long obedience
quote: Your faithfulness is not wasted. It is doing work you cannot see.
body:
As the rain comes down and waters the earth. The verse does not promise you will see the return. It promises the return is happening.

I have planted a lot of things that did not look like they took. The emails I sent that went nowhere. The conversations I had that seemed to change nothing. The prayers that felt like talking to a ceiling. I wanted visible growth, and sometimes there was none. That did not mean nothing was happening.

Rain does not announce itself. It just works. By the time you notice the green, the rain has already finished its job and moved on.

Today: do the unseen work. Trust the slow return.

title: The table
quote: You did not earn the table. You were invited to it.
body:
You prepare a table before me. The verse does not say I set the table. Someone else set it. I just sat down.

I have a long history of trying to earn my seat at a table that was already set for me. I brought dishes nobody asked me to bring. I rearranged chairs. I apologized for being there. None of it was the posture the table was designed for.

The posture is received. Sit. Eat. Be fed. The table is a gift, not a performance review.

Today: stop rearranging what was prepared. Sit down.

title: The still voice
quote: God was not in the earthquake. God was in the small quiet that came after.
body:
Not in the wind. Not in the earthquake. Not in the fire. A still small voice.

I used to want God to show up loud. A sign big enough that I could not miss it. Certainty I could point at. Then the dramatic moment passed and I realized I had been standing in the still voice the whole time, waiting for a different one.

God speaks in the quiet most often. The unrushed morning. The sentence that arrives before you have finished your coffee. The thought you almost dismissed because it did not sound important enough.

Today: be quiet long enough to hear what has been speaking the whole time.

title: The yoke
quote: A yoke is not the absence of weight. It is a way to carry it without breaking.
body:
Come to me all you who are weary. The verse does not promise the weight goes away. It promises a different way of carrying.

I spent years believing rest meant no responsibility. Then I read the verse again and saw what Jesus actually offered. Not the removal of the yoke. A better yoke. One shaped for two.

You are carrying something. Maybe it is the thing that has been on you for a long time. Maybe it is a newer thing. Either way, you were not built to carry it alone with a yoke shaped only for you.

Today: find the second shoulder. Let something be shared.

title: The hidden work
quote: The work that counts is often the work nobody is watching.
body:
Your life is hidden with Christ in God. That is the verse. I used to read it and feel vaguely uncomfortable, like I was being asked to accept obscurity.

Then I started to see it the other way. Hidden is not a demotion. Hidden is a location. It is where the real work gets done, before anyone hands you a microphone or tells you it counts. The years you think are invisible are the years something is being formed that will hold up later when visibility comes.

Most of what made me was not photographed. It did not trend. It happened in rooms nobody saw. If God has you in one of those rooms, do not assume you are being forgotten. You are being built.

Today: do the hidden thing. Let it count without a witness.

title: The small yes
quote: You do not have a big-faith problem. You have a small-yes problem.
body:
Whoever is faithful in very little is also faithful in much.

I wanted the big yes. The launch, the call, the moment I would know for sure. I kept waiting for a door to open wide enough that I would not have to guess whether to walk through it. That door did not come. What came was the small yes, over and over, and the quiet question of whether I was going to take it seriously.

The small yes is the text you finally send. The apology you stop rehearsing and actually say. The fifteen minutes you give to the prayer you keep postponing. The one honest sentence to your wife instead of the managed version. These do not feel like anything. They feel like nothing. They are not nothing.

Faithfulness is not built in the moments you expected it to be tested. It is built in the ones you almost did not notice were a test at all.

Today: find the small yes. Say it. Count it.

title: The ordinary hours
quote: The holy is not elsewhere. It is the hour you were going to call boring.
body:
Whatever you do, do it with all your heart, as working for the Lord, not for men.

I used to read that verse as a motivation line. Work harder. Show up better. I think I was reading the wrong word. The word is whatever. The verse does not promise you an impressive assignment. It promises that the unimpressive one counts.

Most of your life will be ordinary hours. The meeting that was not remarkable. The dinner that was the same dinner. The prayer that felt like nothing. These are not the waiting room for the real life. They are the life.

Today: do the next thing with your whole chest. Not because it matters to anyone watching. Because Someone is.

title: The quiet rebuild
quote: Grace does not wait for your readiness. It arrives before you have cleaned up.
body:
This is the quiet theology of the rebuild. You do not earn your way back into the room. You are carried in, and then you are asked to stay.

Today, do the small, unglamorous thing. Count it. Go again tomorrow. The foundation is being laid whether you can see it or not. That is not a metaphor. That is the mechanism.

title: Grace before readiness
quote: God did not wait until you had your life together. He moved while you were still making the mess.
body:
The verse does not say while we were trying. It says while we were still sinners. That is not a metaphor. That is a timing detail.

Grace is not a reward for reaching a certain temperature of readiness. It is what arrives in the cold room before the heat comes back on. I spent years trying to clean up before I came. The cleanup was not the requirement. It was the avoidance.

Today: let something be enough before it is finished.

title: What the body carried
quote: The body does not lie the way the mind can. It keeps a different kind of record.
body:
Fearfully and wonderfully made. I read this verse for years as a fact about biology. It took me a long time to understand it as a fact about memory.

The body kept every room it was in. It kept the weight, the held breath, the years of tension that lived in the shoulders and the clenched jaw at 2am. It also kept the morning the stillness arrived in a coastal town near the water. Your body has been a witness this entire time.

Today: listen to it. It is telling you something you have been talking over.

title: Not resilience. Faithfulness.
quote: Resilience is about bouncing. Faithfulness is about staying.
body:
The verse says do not lose heart. Not do not feel the weight. Not pretend it is not heavy. Just do not lose heart.

There is a difference between resilience and faithfulness that does not get talked about enough. Resilience is a bounce. Faithfulness is a decision not to leave. The man who keeps showing up to a marriage he damaged is not resilient. He is faithful. Those are different muscles and they are built in different ways.

Today: do not try to bounce. Just stay.

title: Dwell with knowledge
quote: To dwell with knowledge means to actually see the person you are sharing the room with.
body:
The instruction is harder than most of what gets preached. Not just live with her. Dwell. According to knowledge. That word means to inhabit the space with full attention to who is in it.

I was in a lot of rooms I was not actually in. The presence was physical. The attention was somewhere behind a screen, or a glass, or a ceiling I was staring at. My wife named it before I could. Not in an argument. In a quiet sentence that did not raise its voice because it did not have to.

Today: look at the person in the room with you. Not at the room. At the person.

title: Cost as evidence
quote: Love is not a feeling. It is what it costs the person who chose it.
body:
Greater love has no one than this. The verse points at the cost, not the feeling. What someone paid is the evidence of what they chose.

I have watched someone pay a cost I am not sure I could have paid in the same position. Not in one large gesture. In the small daily ones. In the patience she did not have to extend. In the morning she showed up anyway.

That is the verse made flesh. Today: notice what someone is paying for you. Name it. Even just to yourself.

title: The number that was never yours
quote: The number on your paycheck taught you how to think about yourself. That was never the number's job.
body:
As a man thinks in his heart, so is he. I read this for years as a verse about positive thinking. Then I understood it as a warning about who I had let do the thinking.

The quiet assessment that ran underneath everything, the arithmetic about who I was and what I was worth, was not from God. It was from a pay stub. From a title. From an institution that had a number for my contribution and that number became my measure.

You are not the number they gave you. Today: question the source of the voice doing the math.

title: New every morning
quote: It is of the Lord's mercies that we are not consumed. Every morning is a new landing.
body:
They are new every morning. Not new every year, or new every major turning point, or new when you finally get it together. Every morning.

The mercy does not accumulate interest. It does not carry a balance from yesterday. It arrives fresh, today, regardless of what yesterday looked like. I have had mornings where the mercy was the only thing that arrived. No plan, no clarity, no momentum. Just the mercy showing up before anything else.

That turned out to be enough. Today: let the morning be the reset, not the reckoning.
